code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about artists (or related entities) into a fluent description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def _clean(value: str) -> str:
        """Strip surrounding quotes and whitespace."""
        return value.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The placeholder {obj} will be replaced by the cleaned object string.
    pred_map = {
        "birthDate": "was born on {obj}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        "genre": "plays {obj}",
        "instrument": "plays the {obj}",
        "occupation": "is a {obj}",
        "nationality": "is {obj}",
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": "performed with {obj}",
        "recordLabel": "is signed to {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started their career in {obj}",
        "activeYearsEndYear": "ended their career in {obj}",
        "deathDate": "died on {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "alternativeName": "is also known as {obj}",
        "stylisticOrigin": "has its stylistic origins in {obj}",
        "musicFusionGenre": "is fused with {obj}",
        "musicSubgenre": "is a subgenre of {obj}",
        "derivative": "derives from {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "governingBody": "is governed by {obj}",
        "leader": "has leader {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "country": "belongs to the country {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "is owned by {obj}",
        "bandMember": "has band member {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "anthem": "has anthem {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the currency {obj}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "distributingCompany": "is distributed by {obj}",
        "training": "was trained at {obj}",
        "populationDensity": "has a population density of {obj}",
        "meaning": "means \"{obj}\"",
        "longName": "full name is \"{obj}\"",
        "areaTotal": "covers an area of {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies at {obj} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {obj}",
    }

    # Group clauses by their subject to allow multiple subjects in the input.
    subject_clauses: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subj = t.subject
        pred = t.predicate
        obj = _clean(t.object)

        template = pred_map.get(pred, f"{pred} {obj}")
        # If we used the generic fallback, keep the object as is.
        clause = template.format(obj=obj) if "{obj}" in template else template
        subject_clauses.setdefault(subj, []).append(clause)

    sentences: list[str] = []
    for subj, clauses in subject_clauses.items():
        # Join clauses with commas and an 'and' before the last clause.
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            body = clauses[0]
        else:
            body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
        sentence = f"{subj} {body}."
        sentences.append(sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:])

    # Combine sentences for different subjects with a space.
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    subject = triples[0].subject
    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        pred = t.predicate
        obj = t.object
        # Simple natural language mappings for common artist predicates
        if pred == "birthDate":
            clauses.append(f"was born on {obj}")
        elif pred == "birthYear":
            clauses.append(f"was born in {obj}")
        elif pred == "birthPlace":
            clauses.append(f"was born in {obj}")
        elif pred == "origin":
            clauses.append(f"originates from {obj}")
        elif pred == "genre":
            clauses.append(f"plays {obj}")
        elif pred == "instrument":
            clauses.append(f"plays the {obj}")
        elif pred == "occupation":
            clauses.append(f"is a {obj}")
        elif pred == "nationality":
            clauses.append(f"is {obj}")
        elif pred == "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist":
            clauses.append(f"is associated with {obj}")
        elif pred == "recordLabel":
            clauses.append(f"is signed to {obj}")
        elif pred == "activeYearsStartYear":
            clauses.append(f"started their career in {obj}")
        elif pred == "activeYearsEndYear":
            clauses.append(f"ended their career in {obj}")
        elif pred == "deathDate":
            clauses.append(f"died on {obj}")
        elif pred == "deathPlace":
            clauses.append(f"died in {obj}")
        elif pred == "alternativeName":
            clauses.append(f"is also known as {obj}")
        else:
            clauses.append(f"{pred} {obj}")
    # Combine clauses into a fluent sentence
    sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(clauses) + "."
    # Ensure the first character is capitalized
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
